raw_consumer_statement:
A few months ago, I was trying to change travel plans with XXXX XXXX due to illness. I XXXX  for a customer service number for XXXX XXXX and called the number XXXX provided. 

The person I spoke to claimed to be a XXXX representative and charged me nearly {$700.00} for the option to cancel my flight and receive the remainder of the cost back. As this was way less than the total cost of the flight, I agreed, despite my misgivings. I immediately realized something didn't feel right, and asked them to cancel the transaction, but they refused. 

I later found a different number for XXXX on the official website, and found out that cancelations are free, and that I had been scammed. The representative worked for some kind of travel agency pretending to be XXXX ; I received absolutely nothing in exchange for my ~ {$700.00}. 

I immediately called Citi, my credit card company, to dispute the charge and let them know I had been defrauded. Weeks later I received a notification that they would not reimburse me for the fraudulent charge. I spoke to a Citi representative who understood the situation and told me she would take care of it and secure my reiumbursement. Again, several weeks later, I received a notification that they are still refusing to reiumburse what is quite clearly fraud. I challenged it yet again, provided proof that I had received nothing in exchange for the {$700.00}, no good or service, and was denied a third time despite the merchant not providing anything that refuted my claims. 

I have provided Citi detailed documentation of this fraud, including copies of an email the company sent me with a XXXX logo in their signature, as well as my immediate request to cancel the transaction. At this point, my only recourse is to continue submitting my claim through the same process that has now denied me three times. 

Furthermore, I am not the only one who has suffered this exact scam there are news articles ( which I also provided to Citi ) about this exact phenomenon.
